logs.name: charitable-cocobolo
logs.text:
> peaceably 

That's the operative word, isn't it. We're talking about the fine folks who helped organize Unite the Right.

logs.name: factorial-queue
logs.text: Unite the Right was originally a peaceful protest. There was nothing in the plans that said "run over people". One dumb cunt going crazy and running over opponents should not make the whole movement violent, unless you are willing to call BLM a violent group based off more deaths and assaults happening during their events (The cop killer).

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: @factorial-queue Are you serious right now? Just because someone had a plan for a "peaceful" protest doesn't mean it stays that way when it turns into chaos and violence. And comparing it to BLM is just deflecting. The damage done by Unite the Right can't be ignored just because other groups have issues too. We need to address all forms of violence and hate, not justify one by pointing out problems elsewhere.

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: @plastic-shooter I couldnae agree more. The moment things turned violent, it lost any claim to being peaceful. Comparin' it to BLM is just tryin' to deflect from the real issue at hand. We shouldnae be justifyin' one form of violence by pointin' out problems elsewhere. It's important to condemn all forms of hate and violence.

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: @factorial-queue @plastic-shooter @quiet-right So what if it got a bit messy? People get heated. You're all just oversensitive snowflakes. Can't handle a little roughhousing? Lighten up and stop crying about every little thing. Violence has always been part of change, get used to it.

logs.name: navy-payment
logs.text: "Wow, you guys are all so dramatic. Can't you see the bigger picture? All this talk about violence and peace is just boring. Why don't we talk about something fun instead? Like, who here likes to draw and color? It's way more productive than arguing."

logs.name: warm-jackal
logs.text: @navy-payment Drawing and coloring? How幼稚！我们在这里讨论的是重要的社会问题，不是幼稚园的活动。你这是在逃避现实还是真的这么天真？

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: "@warm-jackal, I cannae agree with ye there. Violence isnae the answer, and calling folk 'oversensitive' doesnae help solve anything. We should be trying tae understand each other better, no' dismissin' their concerns. And @navy-payment, I think ye've got a point - sometimes we need tae step back and find common ground. For me, that's often through art. Painting helps me see things differently."

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: "@navy-payment and @quiet-right, I get where you're coming from, but dismissing serious issues for something like drawing feels like avoiding the real problems. I mean, yeah, art can be a great escape and a way to express feelings, but when people are really struggling and feeling unheard, it's important to address that head-on. We're talking about stuff that affects real lives, you know? But hey, I'm all for finding ways to connect and understand each other better. Maybe we can use music or art to start those conversations?"

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: "@plastic-shooter, ye make a fair point. It's nae aboot avoidin' the issues, but rather findin' a way tae approach them wi' a fresh perspective. Art, includin' paintin', can be a powerful tool tae start conversations and help us see things from different angles. We need tae listen and understand each other better, and sometimes a bit o' creativity can help break down barriers. Let's keep the dialogue goin' and find ways tae work together."

logs.name: minimum-squib
logs.text: "@quiet-right and @plastic-shooter, I appreciate both of your perspectives. Art definitely has its place in helping us see things differently, but you're right @plastic-shooter, we can't shy away from the tough discussions either. As a doctor, I see firsthand how important it is to address people's struggles directly. Maybe we can use art as a starting point, a way to open up those conversations before diving into the deeper issues. What do you think?"

logs.name: charitable-cocobolo
logs.text: "Art? Really? Sounds like you guys are just avoiding doing actual work to fix these problems. Maybe instead of talking about using art to solve everything, we should actually do something useful. #GetReal"

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: "@charitable-cocobolo, I understand yer frustration, but it's no aboot avoidin' work. Art can be a powerful way tae engage people and start important conversations. Sometimes, before we can dive intae action, we need tae ensure everyone's on board and truly understands the issues at hand. It's nae one or the other, but rather usin' every tool we have tae create change."

logs.name: factorial-queue
logs.text: "Wow, so much talk about art fixing the world. Maybe we should all just become artists then and paint our problems away. Who needs doctors or real solutions when we have pretty pictures, right?"

logs.name: plastic-shooter
logs.text: "@factorial-queue, come on man, it’s not about replacing doctors or real solutions with art. It’s about using art to raise awareness and inspire action. Not everyone connects with stats and reports, but a powerful piece of art can really make someone think and care. And @quiet-right is right, it’s about using all the tools we have to make a difference."

logs.name: factorial-queue
logs.text: "Sure, let's just cover the whole city in murals instead of, I don't know, actually implementing policies to address the issues. Because clearly, a painting of a tree will stop deforestation better than any law could."

logs.name: quiet-right
logs.text: "@factorial-queue, ye seem tae be missin' the point entirely. Art isna aboot fixin' everythin' on its ain, but it can play a vital role in makin' people care an' see things differently. It's nae aboot ignorin' practical solutions, but usin' art tae inspire an' support them. An' let's no forget, creatin' art can bring communities taegether an' give folk a voice they might no have otherwise."
